behaviors.description: Renamed files are reported only in the `renamed` category, not double-counted as `added` + `deleted`.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary includes real `git` rename detection plus the CLI’s parsing/classification. A mocked diff can miss real failures from git flags/similarity thresholds and subprocess output shape.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows one rename and zero added/deleted for that change.

behaviors.description: Binary-file diffs do not crash the tool, and binary files are included in counts.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling failures typically occur at real git subprocess/stdout decoding boundaries. Mocked text diff fixtures can hide encoding and binary-marker behavior.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit a binary file, then modify/add/delete binary files and run the CLI. Assert process exits successfully and report includes expected binary-file changes.

behaviors.description: On a repo with no commits, the tool returns an empty report instead of an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (subprocess exit/status/messages). Mocking can hide real command-failure handling issues.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit (or defined non-error exit) and structured empty report output.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s true change history.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on end-to-end interaction with real git history traversal and CLI aggregation logic. Module-only tests miss command invocation/range/format mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run CLI for the target range, and assert exact category counts match the constructed ground truth.

behaviors.description: Output is identical when analyzing equivalent staged vs unstaged changes.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real index vs working-tree semantics in git and how the CLI invokes git for each mode. Mocked adapters can miss index/worktree edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create the same logical edits twice: once unstaged, once staged. Run CLI in both states and assert identical structured output.
